extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon won the Stage 5 ITT, while BERNAL Egan surprisingly finished only 15 seconds back to move into 2nd overall at 19 seconds.
tactical_patterns: BERNAL already 15 seconds off ITT winner despite climbing specialty reveals transforming GC versatility for multi-week races.
Education First placed four riders in top-10 ITT positions, suggesting deep squad time-trialing investment beyond van Garderen.
KWIATKOWSKI extends GC lead in flat ITT despite climbing specialist profile, 14 bonus seconds from earlier aggression proving decisive.
QUINTANA loses over minute in flat ITT to slip 17th, confirming extreme vulnerability in non-mountain disciplines persists.

rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: finished 15 seconds behind specialist Yates in flat ITT and moved to 2nd overall, suggesting all-around GC evolution
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: first professional ITT victory while targeting Giro reveals peak form despite claiming course unsuitable for climbers
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: negative: finished 8th despite being pre-stage favorite and Luxembourg TT champion, recovering from 11th at intermediate checkpoint
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: dropped 1:15 to Yates but extended overall lead through earlier bonus seconds, survives rather than dominates
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: finished 17th and dropped over minute overall, now trails by 1+ minute confirming time-trial liability
